# Fixture: dual-axis-time
## Task
Dual-axis time-series: training loss (left axis, log y) + cosine LR schedule (right axis).
## Inputs
- data.json: {steps, loss, lr}
## Outputs
- figure.pdf, figure.png; caption.tex; gen_figure.py
## Hints
- Color-couple axis ticks/labels to their line.
- Avoid using both axes for a "more is better" sense without telling the reader.

content.thinking:
Now I have all the data I need. Let me create the figure generation script. The brief asks for:
1. Dual-axis time-series: training loss (left axis, log y) + cosine LR schedule (right axis)
2. Color-couple axis ticks/labels to their line
3. Avoid using both axes for a "more is better" sense without telling the reader

content.text: Now I'll write the generation script and produce the figure.

input.content:
import json
import numpy as np
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use("Agg")
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as ticker

with open("data.json") as f:
    d = json.load(f)

steps = np.array(d["steps"])
loss  = np.array(d["loss"])
lr    = np.array(d["lr"])

LOSS_COLOR = "#1f77b4"   # blue
LR_COLOR   = "#d62728"   # red

fig, ax1 = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 4))

# --- left axis: training loss (log scale) ---
l1, = ax1.semilogy(steps, loss, color=LOSS_COLOR, linewidth=1.6, label="Train loss")
ax1.set_xlabel("Step", fontsize=11)
ax1.set_ylabel("Training loss (log scale)", color=LOSS_COLOR, fontsize=11)
ax1.tick_params(axis="y", labelcolor=LOSS_COLOR)
ax1.yaxis.set_major_formatter(ticker.FuncFormatter(lambda y, _: f"{y:g}"))
ax1.set_xlim(steps[0], steps[-1])

# --- right axis: learning rate ---
ax2 = ax1.twinx()
l2, = ax2.plot(steps, lr * 1e3, color=LR_COLOR, linewidth=1.4,
               linestyle="--", label="LR (×10⁻³)")
ax2.set_ylabel("Learning rate (×10⁻³)", color=LR_COLOR, fontsize=11)
ax2.tick_params(axis="y", labelcolor=LR_COLOR)
ax2.set_ylim(bottom=0)

# note: loss decreases (lower = better) while LR also decreases by design;
# a legend note clarifies the directional relationship
lines = [l1, l2]
labels = [l.get_label() for l in lines]
ax1.legend(lines, labels, loc="upper right", fontsize=9,
           framealpha=0.85,
           title="↓ loss = better   LR decays by schedule",
           title_fontsize=7.5)

ax1.set_title("Training loss and cosine LR schedule", fontsize=12, pad=8)
fig.tight_layout()

fig.savefig("figure.pdf", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
fig.savefig("figure.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
fig.savefig("figure.svg", bbox_inches="tight")
print("Saved figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg")

import json
import numpy as np
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use("Agg")
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as ticker

with open("data.json") as f:
    d = json.load(f)

steps = np.array(d["steps"])
loss  = np.array(d["loss"])
lr    = np.array(d["lr"])

LOSS_COLOR = "#1f77b4"   # blue
LR_COLOR   = "#d62728"   # red

fig, ax1 = plt.subplots(figsize=(7, 4))

# --- left axis: training loss (log scale) ---
l1, = ax1.semilogy(steps, loss, color=LOSS_COLOR, linewidth=1.6, label="Train loss")
ax1.set_xlabel("Step", fontsize=11)
ax1.set_ylabel("Training loss (log scale)", color=LOSS_COLOR, fontsize=11)
ax1.tick_params(axis="y", labelcolor=LOSS_COLOR)
ax1.yaxis.set_major_formatter(ticker.FuncFormatter(lambda y, _: f"{y:g}"))
ax1.set_xlim(steps[0], steps[-1])

# --- right axis: learning rate ---
ax2 = ax1.twinx()
l2, = ax2.plot(steps, lr * 1e3, color=LR_COLOR, linewidth=1.4,
               linestyle="--", label="LR (×10⁻³)")
ax2.set_ylabel("Learning rate (×10⁻³)", color=LR_COLOR, fontsize=11)
ax2.tick_params(axis="y", labelcolor=LR_COLOR)
ax2.set_ylim(bottom=0)

# note: loss decreases (lower = better) while LR also decreases by design;
# a legend note clarifies the directional relationship
lines = [l1, l2]
labels = [l.get_label() for l in lines]
ax1.legend(lines, labels, loc="upper right", fontsize=9,
           framealpha=0.85,
           title="↓ loss = better   LR decays by schedule",
           title_fontsize=7.5)

ax1.set_title("Training loss and cosine LR schedule", fontsize=12, pad=8)
fig.tight_layout()

fig.savefig("figure.pdf", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
fig.savefig("figure.png", dpi=150, bbox_inches="tight")
fig.savefig("figure.svg", bbox_inches="tight")
print("Saved figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg")
